message:
fix(buf,io,fault,quic,process): Take advance bounds, ReadBuf overflow, fault exclusivity, QUIC round-robin allocation, and process reap strategy

src/bytes/buf/take.rs:
- Fix Take::advance() to check cnt against remaining() (min of limit
  and inner remaining) instead of just limit, preventing advance past
  the inner buffer's actual data when limit exceeds inner length
- Add test_take_advance_when_limit_exceeds_inner_remaining and
  test_take_advance_panics_when_count_exceeds_effective_remaining

src/io/read_buf.rs:
- Fix ReadBuf::advance() to check n against remaining() instead of
  (initialized - filled), preventing wrapping overflow when n is very
  large (e.g. usize::MAX)
- Add read_buf_advance_rejects_oversized_step_without_wrapping test
  with panic::catch_unwind validation

src/channel/fault.rs:
- Make reorder and duplication mutually exclusive per send: a single
  send attempt that triggers reorder no longer also triggers duplication,
  preventing buffered sends from leaking eager duplicates that arrive
  before the reordered original
- Add regression test: reorder_and_duplication_are_mutually_exclusive

src/net/quic_native/streams.rs:
- Replace Vec::collect + index arithmetic in next_writable_stream()
  with BTreeMap::range() iterator chaining, eliminating O(n) allocation
  on every poll and making round-robin cursor advancement O(log n)
- Fix cursor advancement to use Bound::Excluded/Included for correct
  wrap-around semantics

src/process.rs:
- Refactor kill-on-drop reap into explicit 3-strategy dispatch:
  BlockingPool (preferred), DetachedThread (Cx/runtime present but pool
  shutdown), DirectWait (no runtime context)
- Add blocking_pool_for_kill_on_drop_reap() that checks both Cx and
  Runtime handles with is_shutdown() guards
- Add tests for all three strategy paths and shutdown-pool fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
